introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.p1: Must-b is a next-generation, locally hosted autonomous AI agent ecosystem. It transitions AI from being a passive chatbot in a browser tab to an active, autonomous digital workforce operating directly on your machine.
introduction.orchestratorDesc: Must-b changes the game by acting as the Orchestrator of your entire workflow. You provide a high-level goal. Must-b dynamically spawns a swarm of specialized AI agents, maps out a parallel execution graph, controls your terminal, edits your local files, navigates the web via automated browsers, and enforces strict security protocols—all without human intervention.
features: Multi-Agent Swarms (SwarmCoordinator): Spawns isolated PM, Frontend, Backend, and QA agents that work in parallel and communicate natively.
DAG Workflow Engine: Executes complex plans with parallel branching, rollback capabilities, and fault tolerance.
The Shield Protocol (Hookify): Write natural language rules (e.g., 'never delete databases') that instantly convert to runtime execution blocks.
200+ Native & Assimilated Skills: Ships with a massive arsenal of tools for GitHub PR reviews, codebase indexing, web scraping, and terminal execution.
Memory & Lifecycle: HNSW-backed vector store (LTM) with semantic search, plus Ghost Guard (RAM/CPU monitoring) and Night Owl (background execution).
8-Language UI: Fluent in English, Turkish, German, French, Spanish, Portuguese, Japanese, and Chinese.

corePhilosophy.title: Core Philosophy
corePhilosophy.p1: Must-b is built on a singular, uncompromising premise: Artificial Intelligence should not be a passive conversational partner trapped in a browser tab; it must be a sovereign digital workforce integrated directly into your operating system.
corePhilosophy.p2: To achieve zero latency in thought and absolute sovereignty in execution, Must-b employs a strict "Cloud Brain, Local Muscle" architectural paradigm.

cloudBrain.title: 1. The Cloud Brain (Cognitive Offloading)
cloudBrain.p1: Complex reasoning, multi-step execution planning, high-dimensional token processing, and Omni-Context memory retrieval are entirely offloaded to decentralized LLM infrastructures.
list: Dynamic Neural Routing: Must-b automatically routes tasks to the most capable model based on the context (e.g., Claude 3.5 Sonnet for deep code synthesis, Groq for rapid, low-latency decision trees).
DAG Generation: A high-level human intent is mathematically broken down into a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of parallel sub-tasks, ensuring fault-tolerant, concurrent execution.

localMuscle.title: 2. The Local Muscle (Bare-Metal Execution)
localMuscle.p1: The physical manifestation of tasks occurs strictly on the host machine via the highly privileged Must-b Daemon.
list: OS-Level Domination: Hardware-level mouse event simulation via Win32/Quartz APIs, keystroke injection, and background binary execution.
Stateful Manipulation: Direct read/write access to local file systems, Git repositories, terminal sessions, and legacy GUI software without requiring restrictive APIs.
